title: Oblique Shear Test
aim: To determine the shear strength parameters of rock under oblique loading.
theory: Oblique shear test is used to determine shear strength of rock under inclined loading.
The specimen is subjected to compressive load at an angle.
The applied load produces both normal and shear stresses.
Shear failure occurs along a predefined plane.
The shear and normal components depend on the angle of inclination.
The test helps determine cohesion and angle of internal friction.
It simulates field conditions where stresses act at angles.
Used in slope stability and rock engineering analysis.
procedure: Prepare a rock specimen with smooth surfaces.
Place the specimen in the oblique shear fixture at a known angle.
Position the setup in the compression testing machine.
Increase load until failure occurs along the inclined plane.
Repeat the test for different angles if required.
result: Shear strength parameters of the rock were determined using oblique shear test.
